briefing.headline: Rig up a Football Jig — finesse pre-spawn approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Football Jig is a strong choice for pre-spawn with stained water and post frontal conditions.

Wheeler: Separate the two claws on a Punch Out Craw trailer for a football jig, then hop, drag, and deadstick it along the bottom; in stained water the size and radical claw movement is an ideal combination.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Separate the two claws on a Punch Out Craw trailer for a football jig, then hop, drag, and deadstick it along the bottom; in stained water the size and radical claw movement is an ideal combi
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on rock, grass, bluff. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Hackney: 'Use a 3/4 to 1-ounce football head jig on 12-17 pound fluorocarbon to cover water and locate sweet spots along gravel bars or ridges with isolated stumps. Same slow presentation as a Carolin
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on rock, grass, bluff. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Wheeler: When fishing a football jig on deep rock, make a long cast, let it hit bottom, and drag it slowly — most bites come when the bait pops off a rock as bass follow it on the drag

KVD: In cold/post-frontal pre-spawn, don't just wind the Thunder Cricket. Work it like a jig — hop, twitch, pull-and-stop. Get it down in the grass and on the bottom. Bites are extremely subtle: the bait just gets heavy or the vibration stops. 'I ...

Palaniuk: 'I call it floating a crankbait -- pick a crankbait that dives deeper than the depth you're fishing, reel it so it crawls up and over rocks, then back down the other side. It looks like a crawfish slowly scooting.'
Robertson: In Florida grass (pencil reeds, flat reeds, Kissimmee grass, hyacinth), drop the trolling motor and cover water with swimbaits rather than sitting on one spot.

briefing.depthStrategy: Post-frontal pre-spawn fish pull back to 6-12ft staging areas. They won't commit shallow after the front. Target the first breakline off spawning flats and key on deeper structure near shallow spawning zones.
adjustIf: If skies cloud up and wind picks up, re-introduce moving baits — the reprieve from high pressure activates fish
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If bites are subtle, try a lighter jig weight — slower fall rate can be the difference
